instruction: Create a Blueprint with a Health variable at 100. On BeginPlay, check if Health is greater than 50 with a Branch. If true, print Healthy. If false, print Wounded.
expected_dsl:
BLUEPRINT: BP_HealthCheck
PARENT: Actor

VAR Health: Float = 100.0

GRAPH: EventGraph

NODE n1: Event_BeginPlay
NODE n2: GetVar [Variable=Health]
NODE n3: GreaterThan [B=50.0]
NODE n4: Branch
NODE n5: PrintString [InString="Healthy"]
NODE n6: PrintString [InString="Wounded"]

EXEC n1.Then -> n4.Execute
EXEC n4.True -> n5.Execute
EXEC n4.False -> n6.Execute

DATA n2.Value -> n3.A [Float]
DATA n3.ReturnValue -> n4.Condition [Bool]
actual_dsl:
BLUEPRINT: BP_HealthCheck
PARENT: Actor
VAR Health: Float = 100.0
GRAPH: EventGraph
NODE n1: Event_BeginPlay
NODE n2: VariableGet [VarName=Health]
NODE n3: GreaterThan [B=50.0]
NODE n4: Branch
NODE n5: PrintString [InString="Healthy"]
NODE n6: PrintString [InString="Wounded"]
EXEC n1.Then -> n4.Execute
DATA n2.Value -> n3.A [Float]
DATA n3.ReturnValue -> n4.Condition [Bool]
EXEC n4.True -> n5.Execute
EXEC n4.False -> n6.Execute
raw_output:
BLUEPRINT: BP_HealthCheck
PARENT: Actor
VAR Health: Float = 100.0
GRAPH: EventGraph
NODE n1: Event_BeginPlay
NODE n2: VariableGet [VarName=Health]
NODE n3: GreaterThan [B=50.0]
NODE n4: Branch
NODE n5: PrintString [InString="Healthy"]
NODE n6: PrintString [InString="Wounded"]
EXEC n1.Then -> n4.Execute
DATA n2.Value -> n3.A [Float]
DATA n3.ReturnValue -> n4.Condition [Bool]
EXEC n4.True -> n5.Execute
EXEC n4.False -> n6.Execute

instruction: Make a Blueprint with an actor array called Targets. On BeginPlay, use ForEachLoop to iterate through all targets and print each one's display name.
expected_dsl:
BLUEPRINT: BP_ListTargets
PARENT: Actor

VAR Targets: Array<Actor>

GRAPH: EventGraph

NODE n1: Event_BeginPlay
NODE n2: GetVar [Variable=Targets]
NODE n3: ForEachLoop
NODE n4: GetDisplayName
NODE n5: PrintString

EXEC n1.Then -> n3.Execute
EXEC n3.LoopBody -> n5.Execute

DATA n2.Value -> n3.Array [Array]
DATA n3.ArrayElement -> n4.Object [Actor]
DATA n4.ReturnValue -> n5.InString [String]
actual_dsl:
BLUEPRINT: BP_ListTargets
PARENT: Actor
VAR Targets: Array<Actor>
GRAPH: EventGraph
NODE n1: Event_BeginPlay
NODE n2: VariableGet [VarName=Targets]
NODE n3: ForEachLoop
NODE n4: GetDisplayName
NODE n5: PrintString
EXEC n1.Then -> n3.Execute
DATA n2.Value -> n3.Array [Array]
EXEC n3.LoopBody -> n5.Execute
DATA n3.ArrayElement -> n4.Target [Actor]
DATA n4.ReturnValue -> n5.InString [String]
raw_output:
BLUEPRINT: BP_ListTargets
PARENT: Actor
VAR Targets: Array<Actor>
GRAPH: EventGraph
NODE n1: Event_BeginPlay
NODE n2: VariableGet [VarName=Targets]
NODE n3: ForEachLoop
NODE n4: GetDisplayName
NODE n5: PrintString
EXEC n1.Then -> n3.Execute
DATA n2.Value -> n3.Array [Array]
EXEC n3.LoopBody -> n5.Execute
DATA n3.ArrayElement -> n4.Target [Actor]
DATA n4.ReturnValue -> n5.InString [String]

instruction: Create a Blueprint that starts a looping timer called Pulse every 2 seconds on BeginPlay. The Pulse event prints Pulse!. After a 10 second delay, clear the Pulse timer and print Stopped.
expected_dsl:
BLUEPRINT: BP_TimedPulse
PARENT: Actor

GRAPH: EventGraph

NODE n1: Event_BeginPlay
NODE n2: SetTimerByFunctionName [FunctionName="Pulse", Time=2.0, Looping=true]
NODE n3: Delay [Duration=10.0]
NODE n4: ClearTimerByFunctionName [FunctionName="Pulse"]
NODE n5: PrintString [InString="Stopped"]
NODE n6: Event_CustomEvent [EventName="Pulse"]
NODE n7: PrintString [InString="Pulse!"]

EXEC n1.Then -> n2.Execute
EXEC n2.Then -> n3.Execute
EXEC n3.Completed -> n4.Execute
EXEC n4.Then -> n5.Execute
EXEC n6.Then -> n7.Execute
actual_dsl:
BLUEPRINT: BP_TimedPulse
PARENT: Actor
GRAPH: EventGraph
NODE n1: Event_BeginPlay
NODE n2: SetTimerByFunctionName [FunctionName="Pulse", Time=2.0, Looping=true]
NODE n3: Delay [Duration=10.0]
NODE n4: ClearTimerByFunctionName [FunctionName="Pulse"]
NODE n5: PrintString [InString="Stopped"]
NODE n6: Event_CustomEvent [EventName=Pulse]
NODE n7: PrintString [InString="Pulse!"]
EXEC n1.Then -> n2.Execute
EXEC n2.Then -> n3.Execute
EXEC n3.Completed -> n4.Execute
EXEC n4.Then -> n5.Execute
EXEC n6.Then -> n7.Execute
raw_output:
BLUEPRINT: BP_TimedPulse
PARENT: Actor
GRAPH: EventGraph
NODE n1: Event_BeginPlay
NODE n2: SetTimerByFunctionName [FunctionName="Pulse", Time=2.0, Looping=true]
NODE n3: Delay [Duration=10.0]
NODE n4: ClearTimerByFunctionName [FunctionName="Pulse"]
NODE n5: PrintString [InString="Stopped"]
NODE n6: Event_CustomEvent [EventName=Pulse]
NODE n7: PrintString [InString="Pulse!"]
EXEC n1.Then -> n2.Execute
EXEC n2.Then -> n3.Execute
EXEC n3.Completed -> n4.Execute
EXEC n4.Then -> n5.Execute
EXEC n6.Then -> n7.Execute

NODE n2: DivideFloat [A=100.0, B=3.0]
NODE n3: AddFloat [B=15.0]
NODE n4: MultiplyFloat [B=2.0]

instruction: Make a Blueprint that on Tick gets the actor location, clamps the X value between -500 and 500, and sets the actor location to the clamped position.
expected_dsl:
BLUEPRINT: BP_ClampedMove
PARENT: Actor

GRAPH: EventGraph

NODE n1: Event_Tick
NODE n2: GetActorLocation
NODE n3: ClampFloat [Min=-500.0, Max=500.0]
NODE n4: MakeVector [Y=0, Z=0]
NODE n5: SetActorLocation

EXEC n1.Then -> n5.Execute

DATA n2.ReturnValue -> n3.A [Float]
DATA n3.ReturnValue -> n4.X [Float]
DATA n4.ReturnValue -> n5.NewLocation [Vector]
actual_dsl:
BLUEPRINT: BP_ClampPosition
PARENT: Actor
GRAPH: EventGraph
NODE n1: Event_Tick
NODE n2: GetActorLocation
NODE n3: BreakVector
NODE n4: ClampFloat [Min=-500.0, Max=500.0]
NODE n5: MakeVector [Y=0, Z=0]
NODE n6: SetActorLocation
EXEC n1.Then -> n6.Execute
DATA n2.ReturnValue -> n3.Input [Vector]
DATA n3.X -> n4.Value [Float]
DATA n4.ReturnValue -> n5.X [Float]
DATA n5.ReturnValue -> n6.NewLocation [Vector]
raw_output:
BLUEPRINT: BP_ClampPosition
PARENT: Actor
GRAPH: EventGraph
NODE n1: Event_Tick
NODE n2: GetActorLocation
NODE n3: BreakVector
NODE n4: ClampFloat [Min=-500.0, Max=500.0]
NODE n5: MakeVector [Y=0, Z=0]
NODE n6: SetActorLocation
EXEC n1.Then -> n6.Execute
DATA n2.ReturnValue -> n3.Input [Vector]
DATA n3.X -> n4.Value [Float]
DATA n4.ReturnValue -> n5.X [Float]
DATA n5.ReturnValue -> n6.NewLocation [Vector]

instruction: Build an actor that patrols between two points. Use a boolean GoingToB to track direction. On a 3-second looping timer, if GoingToB is true, set location to (500,0,100) and flip GoingToB. If false, set location to (-500,0,100) and flip GoingToB.
expected_dsl:
BLUEPRINT: BP_SimplePatrol
PARENT: Actor

VAR GoingToB: Bool = true

GRAPH: EventGraph

NODE n1: Event_BeginPlay
NODE n2: SetTimerByFunctionName [FunctionName="DoMove", Time=3.0, Looping=true]
NODE n3: Event_CustomEvent [EventName="DoMove"]
NODE n4: GetVar [Variable=GoingToB]
NODE n5: Branch
NODE n6: SetActorLocation
NODE n7: SetVar [Variable=GoingToB, Value=false]
NODE n8: SetActorLocation
NODE n9: SetVar [Variable=GoingToB, Value=true]

EXEC n1.Then -> n2.Execute
EXEC n3.Then -> n5.Execute
EXEC n5.True -> n6.Execute
EXEC n6.Then -> n7.Execute
EXEC n5.False -> n8.Execute
EXEC n8.Then -> n9.Execute

DATA n4.Value -> n5.Condition [Bool]
actual_dsl:
BLUEPRINT: BP_Patrol
PARENT: Actor
VAR GoingToB: Bool = true
GRAPH: EventGraph
NODE n1: Event_BeginPlay
NODE n2: SetTimerByFunctionName [FunctionName="Patrol", Time=3.0, Looping=true]
NODE n3: Event_CustomEvent [EventName="Patrol"]
NODE n4: VariableGet [VarName="GoingToB"]
NODE n5: Branch
NODE n6: SetActorLocation [NewLocation=(500,0,100)]
NODE n7: VariableSet [VarName="GoingToB", Value=false]
NODE n8: SetActorLocation [NewLocation=(-500,0,100)]
NODE n9: VariableSet [VarName="GoingToB", Value=true]
EXEC n1.Then -> n2.Execute
EXEC n3.Then -> n5.Execute
DATA n4.Value -> n5.Condition [Bool]
EXEC n5.True -> n6.Execute
EXEC n6.Then -> n7.Execute
EXEC n5.False -> n8.Execute
EXEC n8.Then -> n9.Execute
raw_output:
BLUEPRINT: BP_Patrol
PARENT: Actor
VAR GoingToB: Bool = true
GRAPH: EventGraph
NODE n1: Event_BeginPlay
NODE n2: SetTimerByFunctionName [FunctionName="Patrol", Time=3.0, Looping=true]
NODE n3: Event_CustomEvent [EventName="Patrol"]
NODE n4: VariableGet [VarName="GoingToB"]
NODE n5: Branch
NODE n6: SetActorLocation [NewLocation=(500,0,100)]
NODE n7: VariableSet [VarName="GoingToB", Value=false]
NODE n8: SetActorLocation [NewLocation=(-500,0,100)]
NODE n9: VariableSet [VarName="GoingToB", Value=true]
EXEC n1.Then -> n2.Execute
EXEC n3.Then -> n5.Execute
DATA n4.Value -> n5.Condition [Bool]
EXEC n5.True -> n6.Execute
EXEC n6.Then -> n7.Execute
EXEC n5.False -> n8.Execute
EXEC n8.Then -> n9.Execute

NODE n7: AddToViewport

EXEC n5.B -> n7.Execute

instruction: Create a moving platform that oscillates up and down using a sine wave. Use a Timer float variable incremented each tick by DeltaSeconds. The Z position is sine of Timer multiplied by 200.

NODE n2: GetVar [Variable=Timer]
NODE n3: AddFloat
NODE n4: SetVar [Variable=Timer]
NODE n5: Sin
NODE n6: MultiplyFloat [B=200.0]
NODE n7: MakeVector [X=0, Y=0]
NODE n8: SetActorLocation

EXEC n1.Then -> n4.Execute
EXEC n4.Then -> n8.Execute

DATA n2.Value -> n3.A [Float]
DATA n1.DeltaSeconds -> n3.B [Float]
DATA n3.ReturnValue -> n4.Value [Float]
DATA n3.ReturnValue -> n5.A [Float]
DATA n5.ReturnValue -> n6.A [Float]
DATA n6.ReturnValue -> n7.Z [Float]
DATA n7.ReturnValue -> n8.NewLocation [Vector]

instruction: Build a collectible system. The Blueprint has ItemCount starting at 0 and RequiredItems at 5. On overlap, increment ItemCount. If ItemCount equals RequiredItems, print All items collected!. Otherwise print the count.

VAR RequiredItems: Int = 5

NODE n2: GetVar [Variable=ItemCount]
NODE n3: AddFloat [B=1]
NODE n4: SetVar [Variable=ItemCount]
NODE n5: GetVar [Variable=RequiredItems]
NODE n6: EqualEqual
NODE n7: Branch
NODE n8: PrintString [InString="All items collected!"]
NODE n9: PrintString

EXEC n1.Then -> n4.Execute
EXEC n4.Then -> n7.Execute
EXEC n7.True -> n8.Execute
EXEC n7.False -> n9.Execute

DATA n3.ReturnValue -> n4.Value [Int]
DATA n3.ReturnValue -> n6.A [Int]
DATA n5.Value -> n6.B [Int]
DATA n6.ReturnValue -> n7.Condition [Bool]
DATA n3.ReturnValue -> n9.InString [String]

instruction: Create a health system Blueprint. Has Health at 100. On overlap begin, start a looping timer DamageLoop every 0.5 seconds. On overlap end, clear the timer. The DamageLoop event subtracts 10 from Health. If Health drops below 0, print Dead and destroy the actor.
expected_dsl:

PARENT: Actor

VAR Health: Float = 100.0

GRAPH: EventGraph

NODE n3: Event_ActorEndOverlap

NODE n5: Event_CustomEvent [EventName="DamageLoop"]
NODE n6: GetVar [Variable=Health]
NODE n7: SubtractFloat [B=10.0]
NODE n8: SetVar [Variable=Health]
NODE n9: LessThan [B=0.0]
NODE n10: Branch
NODE n11: PrintString [InString="Dead"]
NODE n12: DestroyActor

EXEC n1.Then -> n2.Execute
EXEC n3.Then -> n4.Execute
EXEC n5.Then -> n8.Execute
EXEC n8.Then -> n10.Execute
EXEC n10.True -> n11.Execute
EXEC n11.Then -> n12.Execute

DATA n6.Value -> n7.A [Float]
DATA n7.ReturnValue -> n8.Value [Float]
DATA n7.ReturnValue -> n9.A [Float]
